creator: scheidell@FreeBSD.org
text:
Jim:

Thanks for maintaining zabbix-server.  Your help keeping FreeBSD updated 
is appreciated!


the actual patch part of your fix didn't actually get attached (normal 
MIME attachment), or, more likely, GNATS mangled it.

if using command line tools? can you 'reply' to that pr with the patch 
attached?

I would do something like:

elm -A zabbix-server-1.8.11,2.patch -s 'Re: ports/166313: please, update 
net-mgmt/zabbix-server to 1.8.11' bug-followup@FreeBSD.org

(mutt, or, if using gui based tools, maybe disable PGP signature.  seems 
GNATS mungs emails with PGP signature)

its important to do that, even though many (very sr ) committers might 
be able to pull a raw patch from the pr, as you see, your patch has been 
sitting here since march 28th.

Give a shot at trying to attach the patch the the pr reply, and see if 
it shows up as a patch, not an inline email.



-- 
Michael Scheidell, CTO
 >*| * SECNAP Network Security Corporation
d: +1.561.948.2259
w: http://people.freebsd.org/~scheidell
